steps.title: Recognize that surface area scales with the square of linear dimensions
steps.content: When similar solids have linear dimensions (like edge lengths) that scale by a factor $k$, their 2D measurements (like surface area) scale by $k^2$. The edge length of cube Y is $\frac{3}{64}$ times that of cube X.

steps.title: Calculate the surface area ratio
steps.content:
The surface area of cube Y is $\left(\frac{3}{64}\right)^2$ times the surface area of cube X, giving:
$$n = \left(\frac{3}{64}\right)^2 = \frac{9}{4,096}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
